excluded_agency_experts: Ahmed Tigani (commissioner, NYC Department of Buildings; invited testimony, Manhattan Round 2)
David Garfinkel (invited expert, Staten Island Round 2)
Julia Fusfeld (invited expert, Staten Island Round 2)
Kenny Minaya (commissioner, NYC Department of Small Business Services; invited testimony, Queens Round 2)
Seth Bynum (senior advisor to the Deputy Mayor for Housing and Planning; SPEED Task Force, invited testimony, Manhattan Round 2)

sources.Bronx: https://www.nyc.gov/assets/charter/downloads/pdf/Exec-General-Workspace-Charter-Review-Commission-06-10-2026.pdf  (official Veritext stenographic transcript, June 10, 2026, Fordham University/Bronx — NOTE the filename says 'Exec General Workspace' and does not name the borough; it is the Bronx Round 1 hearing. Reconciled 2026-07-20: the Attendee/Speaker roster listed 33 names and matched all 33 catalogued witnesses one-for-one — none missing, none extra. No corrections made: every catalogued name is high-confidence and independently verified, and the steno spellings are consistently worse (DeLone/Dilone, Menia/Minaya, Janessa Jewels/Jenessa Jules, Demaris Sumadiqui/Damaris Zoumanigui, Pascal Undrago/Pascal A. Ouedraogo, Sarah Spyat/Sarah Espillat, Bah, Sajo/Sadio Bah). Speaker labels MR. SHAW and MR. PENJABA are commissioners asking questions, not witnesses.)
sources.Brooklyn: https://www.nyc.gov/assets/charter/downloads/pdf/Exec-General-Workspace-Charter-Review-06-11-2026.pdf  (official Veritext stenographic transcript, June 11, 2026, Brooklyn Law School — same misleading 'Exec General Workspace' filename; it is the Brooklyn Round 1 hearing. Reconciled 2026-07-20: the APPEARANCES + CONTENTS rosters matched all 29 catalogued witnesses one-for-one — none missing, none extra. No corrections made. Steno 'Andrew Ryan' is wrong for the Citizens Budget Commission president Andrew Rein; 'Stacey Vogue' is marked [ph] (phonetic) in the record itself, so the independently-verified catalog name Stacey Bode stands.)
sources.Manhattan: https://www.nyc.gov/assets/charter/downloads/pdf/COGE-MN-Hearing-1-Offical-Transcript-20260617-1.pdf  (official Veritext stenographic transcript, June 9, 2026; reconciled 2026-07-20 — roster matched all 39 catalogued witnesses, none missing, none extra. Corrections: Sam Panuccio -> Samuel Peter Panuccio (documented in his own prior sworn City Council testimony); Henry Sutherland -> Henry Sullivan (his self-introduction in the official record; no public record for either spelling, so confidence lowered to medium). Bria Donohue confirmed correct against AIA NY staff page despite the transcript rendering Maria Donahue; other steno spellings (Tim Hopkins, Gail Brewer, Michael Picarello) are worse than the catalog and were left alone.)
sources.Queens: https://www.nyc.gov/assets/charter/downloads/pdf/2026/COGE-QN-Hearing-1-Official-Transcript-Condensed_20260713.pdf  (official Veritext stenographic transcript, June 22, 2026; re-checked 2026-07-16, roster matched all 25 catalogued names, no changes)
sources.Manhattan (Round 2): https://www.nyc.gov/assets/charter/downloads/pdf/2026/Manhattan-Rd-2-Transcript.pdf  (official Veritext stenographic transcript, July 8, 2026, Riverside Church; posted after 2026-07-21 and reconciled 2026-07-23. The speaker roster matched all 24 catalogued witnesses one-for-one. Corrections: Adria Konz -> Adria Quinones (she self-identifies in the official record; independently confirmed as a senior IT consultant at MetroPlusHealth, so confidence raised to high) and Michelle Knap -> Michelle Napp (the steno reads "Michelle Napp" and her own self-introduction in the YouTube captions reads "Michelle Nappy of Repair Restor"; the "Knap" spelling came only from the chair reading the sign-up list. No public record of either spelling, so confidence stays low). Two further public speakers appear in the record - Comptroller Mark Levine and Citizens Budget Commission president Andrew Rein, both testifying on the rainy day fund - but both are already catalogued as proponents of enforceable-rainy-day-fund-and-honest-budgeting (Levine via his written submission, Rein via Brooklyn Round 1), so the per-idea name dedupe correctly skips them and no rows were added. Excluded as invited administration officials: DOB Commissioner Ahmed Tigani and Seth Bynum of the Deputy Mayor for Housing and Planning.)
sources.Queens (Round 2): https://www.nyc.gov/assets/charter/downloads/pdf/meetings-hearings/7132026-COGE-Queens-2-Meeting-Minutes-pages-deleted-pages.pdf  (official COGE meeting minutes, July 13, 2026, Queens Borough Hall, signed by Secretary Susan Kang; posted between 2026-07-23 and 2026-07-24 and reconciled 2026-07-24. NOTE the PDF is hybrid: pages 1-2 extract as text, page 3 is a scanned image and must be read visually -- extracting text alone silently loses 14 of the 33 speakers. The minutes list 33 speakers, of whom 32 are members of the public; SBS Commissioner Kenny Minaya is an invited agency official and stays excluded. All 32 matched the catalog. Fourteen names were corrected to the minutes spelling after each was confirmed against an independent public record: Alex Kamarda -> Alex Camarda, Alex Moreno -> Alex Morano, Edgar Alfonso -> Edgar Alfonseca, Judy -> Judy Wessler, Kevin Lera -> Kevin LaCherra, May Frank -> Mae Francke, Merrill Labour -> Meryl LaBorde, Michelle Yanchi -> Michelle Yanche, Misha Nonnen -> Misha Nonen, Namaka Gibbi -> Nwamaka Ejebe, Nyla Rosario -> Naila Caicedo-Rosario, Sarah Catalinato -> Sara Catalinotto, Xavier Puok -> Xavier Pu-Folkes, Gail Brewer -> Gale Brewer. Two judgment calls against the minutes: the minutes formalize the former city chief information security officer as 'Geoffrey Brown' but he uses 'Geoff Brown' everywhere publicly, so the short form stands; and the minutes and the captions BOTH misspell the PIST NYC founder, who is Sara Catalinotto (no h). Alex Morano is the one case where the official minutes beat the witness's own self-introduction in the video captions -- 'Moreno' matches no public record, 'Morano' matches his Streetsblog author page and prior Council testimony. Xavier Pu-Folkes keeps low confidence: the minutes spelling is plausible (Pu-Folkes is an established New York surname) but no public record ties it to this witness. Pedro Rodriguez stays in the catalog at medium confidence although the minutes omit him entirely -- the chair announces 'Alex Morano and Pedro Rodriguez, both online participants' at t=6066 and he then testifies, so the minutes, not the catalog, are incomplete. Correcting Gail -> Gale Brewer merged her Queens Round 2 rows with the Gale Brewer already catalogued from Manhattan Round 1, so the distinct-witness total falls 247 -> 246 without anyone being removed.)
